body.title: BoredomDetector WINDOW_MINS 20→5 min
body.result: BoredomDetector WINDOW_MINS 20→5 in push_sources.py; boredom fires 4x faster when idle
body.description:
Reduce BoredomSource.WINDOW_MINS from 20 to 5 minutes in push_sources.py (line 1757). The current 20-min window requires Igor's arousal to be flat for 20 minutes before BoredomSource fires — far too slow for reactive learning. At 5 min, boredom kicks in 4× faster when Igor is genuinely idle. This is complementary to T-ne-desperation-pull-ltm (which is proactive); this makes the reactive path faster. Change the default in the env fallback: int(os.getenv('IGOR_BOREDOM_WINDOW_MINS', '5')).

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/push_sources.py (BoredomSource.WINDOW_MINS constant at line 1757)
**Design rules:** OOP-first (constant in class body, no structural change needed)
**Scope boundary:** IN: WINDOW_MINS default 20→5 (env var still overrides). OUT: AROUSAL_THRESH; BoredomSource push() logic; rolling window mechanics
**Test plan:** No unit test needed. Observe after restart: BoredomSource entries (BOREDOM_DETECTED) appear in TWM within ~5 min of Igor becoming idle; channel message confirms. Compare to prior 20-min baseline.
